fact: November 21 sits exactly 30 days before the winter solstice in the Northern Hemisphere, making it like nature's countdown timer to the shortest day of the year!
In Antarctica, penguin chicks are getting their first real feathers around this time, looking like fluffy gray cotton balls learning to waddle!
The sun rises after 7 AM in most of the northern United States by November 21, and some kids are walking to school in complete darkness!
Maple trees have dropped almost all their leaves by this date in cold regions, creating crunchy leaf piles that can be 3 feet deep!
Cats often seem extra cuddly around November 21 because their winter coats are fully grown in, making them much fluffier than in summer!
The zodiac sign switches from Scorpio to Sagittarius around November 21, which astrologers say means the sky's personality changes from mysterious to adventurous!
In northern Canada and Alaska, the aurora borealis appears almost every clear night by this date because of longer darkness and increased solar activity!
Squirrels have typically buried thousands of nuts by November 21 and can remember where most of them are hidden using smell and spatial memory!
Evergreen trees like pine and spruce slow their growth to almost zero by November 21, entering a deep sleep that lasts until spring!
In the Southern Hemisphere, November 21 brings the smell of blooming jacaranda trees, which drop purple flowers like nature's confetti!
